sections.paragraph:
ἐμβαπτίζω (s. βαπτίζω; Nicander [II b.c.] Fgm. 70, 12 [in Athen. 4, 11, 133d]; Plut., Sulla 466 [21, 8]) = next entry, to immerse or plunge partially in a liquid for a brief time, dip in/into. Mid. dip for oneself (s. ἐμβάπτω) Mk 14:20 D.—S. DELG s.v. βάπτω.

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 321
